title: Amplify Node-Type Templates
description: The system-owned registry of graph node types. Every node in a folded task graph (schemas/task-graph.schema.json) carries a required, explicit `type` that names one of these templates; scripts/task.mjs reads THIS file at load time and validates each node against its type's template. A template names: the properties that are required on a node of that type, an optional set of additional allowed properties, and a per-type executor rule. `executor` and `deps` are required on every type. Property semantics (id/deps/executor grammar, max_attempts integer, etc.) are the same as task-graph.schema.json -- this file only declares which properties belong to which type, plus the executor constraint. The execute-plan dump authors `implement` nodes; `resolve`/`audit`/`reduce` nodes are created at runtime by the lifecycle (audit-resolver registers auditors; the reducer reduces a round). The `reduce` executor names subagent(amplify:audit-reducer), which is NOT yet an authored agent file -- this registry only names it.

implement.description: An implement-and-audit task authored by the execute-plan dump. Its implementer always runs subagent(general-purpose); specialized GUI/behavioral work is an audit node (authored executor), never an implementer.

resolve.description: The audit-resolver subnode for a completed task: it designs the auditor panel. Created at runtime.

audit.description: One auditor that verifies a task's acceptance criteria against the implementer's diff, blind and read-only. Its executor is an authored sub-agent chosen at runtime by the audit-resolver (any value matching the executor grammar).

reduce.description: Reduces one audit round to a verdict, keyed by a counter. Its executor names subagent(amplify:audit-reducer) (registry name only; the agent file does not exist yet).
